pr_title: chore: CI failure readability + wip carryover
title: apps/mobile/e2e-web/flows/journeys/j12-pre-profile-create-profile.spec.ts` has two identical `import { pressableClick } from '../../helpers/pressable'` lines (the first was already at line 2 before th
body: `apps/mobile/e2e-web/flows/journeys/j12-pre-profile-create-profile.spec.ts` has two identical `import { pressableClick } from '../../helpers/pressable'` lines (the first was already at line 2 before this PR; the diff adds a second at line 4). TypeScript strict mode rejects duplicate import bindings (TS2300). The diff should have removed the original import instead of adding a second.

pr_title: chore: CI failure readability + wip carryover
title: Two test files edited in this PR still carry internal `jest.mock()` calls without `gc1-allow` annotations or a deferral noted in the commit message. CLAUDE.md GC6 requires cleanup on every test-file e
body:
Two test files edited in this PR still carry internal `jest.mock()` calls without `gc1-allow` annotations or a deferral noted in the commit message. CLAUDE.md GC6 requires cleanup on every test-file edit, or an explicit deferral recorded in the commit message (file paths + count).

`apps/api/src/middleware/env-validation.test.ts` line 1: `jest.mock('../config', () => ({ ... }))` — no gc1-allow, no deferral noted.

`apps/api/src/routes/dashboard.test.ts` lines 47, 61, 71, 89, 103: `jest.mock('../services/account')`, `../services/profile`, `../services/dashboard`, `../services/auth`, `../services/weekly-report` — all internal, no annotations.

All are pre-existing, so cleanup or deferral in the commit message both satisfy GC6 — but the PR description does not mention them.

---

pr_title: chore: CI failure readability + wip carryover
title: Line 13: `const ANSI = /\[[0-9;]*m/g;
body:
Line 13: `const ANSI = /\[[0-9;]*m/g;`

Without a leading `\x1b` the pattern matches literal bracket sequences in test output that are not escape codes, e.g. a message containing `[0m]` or a Markdown table cell. This corrupts the `firstFailureLine` and `gaEscape` values passed to GitHub annotations. Fix: `/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m/g`.

---

pr_title: chore: CI failure readability + wip carryover
title: GC1 — env-validation.test.ts extends an internal mock
body:
**GC1 — env-validation.test.ts extends an internal mock**

The existing `jest.mock('../config', ...)` is grandfathered, but this PR adds `validateProductionBindings` to it, deepening the mock dependency. The GC1 ratchet is forward-only on new `jest.mock(` calls, so this is technically in bounds — but per GC6 the canonical move is to convert to `jest.requireActual` + targeted overrides since the file is already being touched.

**safeSend — orphaned-promise comment would help future readers**

The timeout path intentionally leaves `sendPromise` running (Inngest/fetch expose no AbortSignal) and relies on the `.catch` inside `sendPromise` to route late rejections into `captureException` via the `timedOut` flag. The implementation is correct; a short inline comment at the timeout branch would prevent the next reader from treating the orphaned promise as a leak.

---

pr_title: chore: CI failure readability + wip carryover
title: GitHub Actions parses annotation metadata as comma-separated `key=value` pairs. A test title containing `=` (e.g. `"expects a = b"`) corrupts the key boundary and truncates or misparses the title fiel
body:
GitHub Actions parses annotation metadata as comma-separated `key=value` pairs. A test title containing `=` (e.g. `"expects a = b"`) corrupts the key boundary and truncates or misparses the title field. Should add `.replace(/=/g, '%3D')`. See inline comment on `scripts/jest-ci-reporter.cjs:66`.

---

pr_title: chore: CI failure readability + wip carryover
title: The guard throws `'seedPendingAuthRedirectForTesting is dev-only'` but the real constraint is `EXPO_PUBLIC_E2E === 'true'`. A plain dev build without that env var throws the same message with no hint
body:
The guard throws `'seedPendingAuthRedirectForTesting is dev-only'` but the real constraint is `EXPO_PUBLIC_E2E === 'true'`. A plain dev build without that env var throws the same message with no hint of what's missing. See inline comment on `apps/mobile/src/lib/pending-auth-redirect.ts:123`.

---

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: Both the PR body and `docs/audit/e2e/p5-execution-status.md` document the local multi-worker flow as:
body:
Both the PR body and `docs/audit/e2e/p5-execution-status.md` document the local multi-worker flow as:

```bash
node apps/mobile/e2e-web/helpers/serve-exported-web.mjs &
CI=1 PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_LOCAL_API=1 ... pnpm run test:e2e:web --workers=2
```

But `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` evaluates to `false` when `CI=1`. Playwright's `reuseExistingServer: false` throws if the URL is already responding — it will spawn `serve-exported-web.mjs`, hit EADDRINUSE, call `process.exit(1)`, and abort before any test runs.

The command must omit `CI=1`:

```bash
PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_LOCAL_API=1 doppler run --project mentomate --config stg -- \
  pnpm run test:e2e:web --workers=2
```

Without `CI=1`, `reuseExistingServer: true` kicks in, Playwright sees the URL already responding, and reuses the external server. The `--workers=2` CLI flag still takes effect. (See inline comment on `playwright.config.ts:73`.)

---

### 🟡 Should-fix: `require()` in `runtime.ts` is untyped and fragile

`runtime.ts` uses a bare `require('./e2e-defaults.js')` (line 20), which only works because Playwright's esbuild compilation injects `require` into TypeScript files. This is invisible to the TypeScript compiler — the `as string` cast on line 23 is the tell. `serve-exported-web.mjs` solves this correctly with `createRequire`.

Simplest fix: convert `e2e-defaults.js` to `e2e-defaults.ts` (it's 7 lines) and use a real `import`. This eliminates the mixed-module shim and the cast. (See inline comment on `runtime.ts:20`.)

---

### 🟡 Should-fix: `import` declaration placed mid-file in `.mjs`

`import { createRequire } from 'node:module'` appears at line 119, after ~115 lines of executable code. ESM hoisting makes this work at runtime, but it will confuse any reader scanning imports. Move to line 1 with the other Node.js imports. (See inline comment on `serve-exported-web.mjs:119`.)

---

### Non-blocking notes

- **`statSync` in the HTTP handler** blocks the event loop on every request. The inline comment explains the tradeoff accurately — for a single-machine local test server with ~30 sequential requests, the event-loop cost is negligible. No action needed, but keeping the comment is the right call.
- **Log files in `docs/audit/e2e/runs/`** (14 files, ~12k lines total) will stay in git history forever. They are useful evidence for the audit trail, but consider whether a linked Notion page or CI artefact would be a better long-term home.
- **`manual-learner-ux.config.ts`** also uses `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` and will inherit correct local behaviour automatically since it reads `apiBaseUrl` from `runtime.ts`. No changes needed there, but worth knowing the blast radius of any future refactor of this pattern.

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: Mixed module system in `runtime.ts`** (see inline on line 20)
body:
**1. Mixed module system in `runtime.ts`** (see inline on line 20)

`runtime.ts` uses ESM-style `import` at the top but calls bare `require()`. This works today because Playwright compiles TypeScript to CJS, but silently breaks under NodeNext/ESM. Fix: promote `e2e-defaults.js` to `e2e-defaults.ts` and use `import`. `serve-exported-web.mjs` keeps `createRequire` since it is a native `.mjs` file. The `as string` cast on line 23 disappears as a side effect.

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: Fragile hostname match in `playwright.config.ts`** (see inline on line 13)
body:
**2. Fragile hostname match in `playwright.config.ts`** (see inline on line 13)

`apiBaseUrl.includes('.workers.dev')` matches any substring of the URL. Use `new URL(apiBaseUrl).hostname.endsWith('.workers.dev')` to target the hostname specifically.

---

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: runtime.ts uses bare require('./e2e-defaults.js') while serve-exported-web.mjs correctly uses createRequire(import.meta.url). This works today because Playwright compiles .ts files via its own CommonJ
body:
runtime.ts uses bare require('./e2e-defaults.js') while serve-exported-web.mjs correctly uses createRequire(import.meta.url). This works today because Playwright compiles .ts files via its own CommonJS transform, but it is inconsistent and fragile:

- Bare require is not defined in the ESM type surface ("module": "esnext" in tsconfig.base.json). The file is excluded from tsconfig.app.json so this escapes type-checking.
- The as string cast on line 23 exists solely to paper over the missing type information from the untyped require call.
- Any future loader change that runs runtime.ts as ESM (e.g., tsx in ESM mode, Playwright ESM mode) will produce a ReferenceError: require is not defined at startup.

**Fix:** Mirror the .mjs pattern — add createRequire to runtime.ts, or convert e2e-defaults.js to e2e-defaults.ts with a named export and use a normal import.

---

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: The CI workflow defaults PLAYWRIGHT_API_URL to https://mentomate-api-stg.zwizzly.workers.dev, so today usesSharedStagingApi is true and CI stays at 1 worker. However, if a repo admin changes the E2E_A
body:
The CI workflow defaults PLAYWRIGHT_API_URL to https://mentomate-api-stg.zwizzly.workers.dev, so today usesSharedStagingApi is true and CI stays at 1 worker. However, if a repo admin changes the E2E_API_URL GitHub variable to https://api-test.mentomate.com, CI silently escalates from 1 worker to 4 — no code change, no diff to review. The PR body documents this as intentional, but .github/workflows/e2e-web.yml does not, so the next person touching that variable won't see the warning.

**Recommendation:** Add a comment to e2e-web.yml documenting that switching E2E_API_URL from *.workers.dev to the custom domain implicitly enables 4 workers and is intentional.

---

pr_title: fix(e2e): P5a — custom domain + web server stabilisation for parallel Playwright workers
title: playwright.config.ts` — `fullyParallel: false` on the `setup` project has no in-code explanation
body:
**`playwright.config.ts` — `fullyParallel: false` on the `setup` project has no in-code explanation**

The new `fullyParallel: false` on the `setup` project silently encodes an invariant (prevents concurrent auth-setup / seed-worker collisions) that lives only in `p5-execution-status.md`. A future maintainer or automated reformatter will see an apparently redundant flag and remove it. Add a single-line comment so the invariant travels with the code.

---

pr_title: Fix remaining mobile flow blockers
title: New relative-path internal jest.mock without a gc1-allow annotation.
body:
File: `apps/mobile/src/components/session/use-session-streaming.test.ts`
Line: replacement `jest.mock('./ChatShell', …)`
Issue: New relative-path internal `jest.mock` without a `gc1-allow` annotation. The PR removes the old `jest.mock('../session', …)` (which carried `// gc1-allow: barrel exports RN components…`) and replaces it with `jest.mock('./ChatShell', …)` — the new line is a fresh GC1 violation that CI lint will reject.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Code Quality Guards — "CI fails any PR that adds a relative-path `jest.mock('./...')` … without `// gc1-allow: <reason>` on the same line"
Suggested Fix: Add `// gc1-allow: ChatShell exports RN animation components that cannot render in JSDOM` to the new `jest.mock('./ChatShell', …)` line

pr_title: Fix remaining mobile flow blockers
title: The command in the receipt ran only use-session-streaming.test.ts, but testFiles tracks 11 changed test files.
body:
File: `.test-receipts/mobile.json`
Line: L13
Issue: The `command` in the receipt ran only `use-session-streaming.test.ts`, but `testFiles` tracks 11 changed test files. The other 10 (`camera.test.tsx`, `progress/[subjectId]/index.test.tsx`, `quiz/launch.test.tsx`, `quiz/play.test.tsx`, `topic/index.test.tsx`, `create-subject.test.tsx`, `session-summary/[sessionId].test.tsx`, `bundle-safety.test.ts`, `SessionMessageActions.test.tsx`, `use-books.test.ts`) have no green attestation in the receipt. The receipt system exists precisely to prevent broken mobile tests from shipping (CLAUDE.md cites PR #257, 189 broken tests). A narrowed command providing a partial attestation defeats the mechanism.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation — "Generate with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` — runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test`"
Suggested Fix: Regenerate the receipt with the standard script: `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile`. If the full run is too slow locally, the PR description's "11 suites, 317 tests" claim should be captured by the receipt, not just asserted in prose.

pr_title: Fix remaining mobile flow blockers
title: The command field shows a focused --runTestsByPath apps/mobile/src/components/session/use-session-streaming.test.ts run
body:
File: `.test-receipts/mobile.json`
Line: L13
Issue: The `command` field shows a focused `--runTestsByPath apps/mobile/src/components/session/use-session-streaming.test.ts` run — only one file was actually tested. Yet the `testFiles` block lists 16 changed test files as "verified", including `quiz/play.test.tsx`, `progress/[subjectId]/index.test.tsx`, `camera.test.tsx`, `quiz/launch.test.tsx`, and others that were not exercised by this command. The receipt also has an **earlier** timestamp (13:28:24Z) than the receipt it replaces on main (14:11:17Z), meaning it predates several of the PR's later changes. CLAUDE.md states the receipt must be generated with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile`, which runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test` and writes the receipt only on green. This guard exists specifically to prevent a repeat of PR #257 (189 broken mobile tests hidden by a focused CI step).
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation — "Mobile test-file changes require a receipt … Generate with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` — runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test`"
Suggested Fix: Regenerate the receipt with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` after all changes are final. The receipt must reflect a green full-suite run, not a focused single-file pass.

pr_title: Fix remaining mobile flow blockers
title: Receipt records "pre-commit related mobile tests; focused SessionMessageActions rerun after rebase" instead of "pnpm exec nx run mobile:test".
body:
File: `.test-receipts/mobile.json`
Line: `"command"` field
Issue: Receipt records `"pre-commit related mobile tests; focused SessionMessageActions rerun after rebase"` instead of `"pnpm exec nx run mobile:test"`. The receipt system was introduced after PR #257 shipped with 189 broken mobile tests that a partial CI run hid. Running only the 11 changed suites means regressions in the other ~244 suites (e.g., suites that import session barrel components that were refactored in this PR) are not caught.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Required Validation — "Generate with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` — runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test`"
Suggested Fix: Regenerate the receipt with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` (full suite) before pushing

pr_title: Fix remaining mobile flow blockers
title: Per-screen error classification is extended: three new branches classify typed error classes by error.name inside the screen layer.
body:
File: `apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/quiz/launch.tsx`
Line: added `if (apiError.name === 'ForbiddenError') return 'FORBIDDEN'` etc.
Issue: Per-screen error classification is extended: three new branches classify typed error classes by `error.name` inside the screen layer. The UX Resilience Rules require classifying errors once at the API client boundary so screens switch on error type, not inspect raw error fields. The existing `errorCodeFrom` function already drifted from this rule, but the diff adds three new name-based branches, deepening the coupling.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § UX Resilience Rules — "Classify errors at the API client boundary, not per-screen"
Suggested Fix: Move `name`-based classification into the API client middleware alongside the existing `ForbiddenError`/`QuotaExceededError` class instantiation, then remove the `name` checks from `errorCodeFrom`

pr_title: Expand thin book topic maps
title: normalizeTopicTitle and prepareTopicExpansion are business logic (topic deduplication, connection filtering, fallback-triggering) defined directly in a route file.
body:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/books.ts`
Line: L55–L120
Issue: `normalizeTopicTitle` and `prepareTopicExpansion` are business logic (topic deduplication, connection filtering, fallback-triggering) defined directly in a route file. Route files must be thin; all business logic belongs in `services/`. This pattern is also enforced by eslint G1/G5.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules
Suggested Fix: Move both functions into a new helper in `apps/api/src/services/curriculum.ts` (or a dedicated `services/curriculum-expand.ts`), export them, and call them from the route handler.

pr_title: Expand thin book topic maps
title: The expandExisting branch puts ~66 lines of business logic directly in the route handler: topic filtering (existing.topics.filter(...).length < MIN_GENERATED_BOOK_TOPICS), expansionContext string cons
body:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/books.ts`
Line: L132–L218
Issue: The `expandExisting` branch puts ~66 lines of business logic directly in the route handler: topic filtering (`existing.topics.filter(...).length < MIN_GENERATED_BOOK_TOPICS`), `expansionContext` string construction, a try/catch around the LLM call with fallback to `buildFallbackBookTopics`, `prepareTopicExpansion`, and `persistBookTopics`. Route handlers must not contain this kind of orchestration or domain decision-making — it belongs in `services/`. This will fail ESLint G1/G5 enforcement.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules
Suggested Fix: Extract a single `expandBookTopics(db, profileId, subjectId, bookId, opts)` service function (e.g. in `services/book-expansion.ts` or `services/curriculum.ts`) that takes the existing topics, does the thin-book check, builds context, calls the LLM, handles the fallback, and persists. The route body then becomes one `await expandBookTopics(...)` call behind the existing `if (expandExisting)` guard.

pr_title: Expand thin book topic maps
title: normalizeTopicTitle is defined identically in both files
body:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/books.ts` L55 / `apps/api/src/services/curriculum.ts` L1120
Line: both
Issue: `normalizeTopicTitle` is defined identically in both files — `title.trim().toLowerCase()`. Duplication is a consequence of the above violation but should be resolved in the same fix.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules
Suggested Fix: Define once in the service and import from there.

pr_title: Expand thin book topic maps
title: prepareTopicExpansion is 66 lines of business logic (topic deduplication, fallback augmentation, connection dedup/filtering) defined in a route file.
body:
File: `apps/api/src/routes/books.ts`
Line: L145–208
Issue: `prepareTopicExpansion` is 66 lines of business logic (topic deduplication, fallback augmentation, connection dedup/filtering) defined in a route file. The G1/G5 lint rules only catch ORM-primitive imports, so this will pass CI, but the architectural rule is clear: business logic belongs in `services/`, not route handlers.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules
Suggested Fix: Move `prepareTopicExpansion` (and the private `normalizeTopicTitle` that feeds it) into `apps/api/src/services/curriculum.ts`, export it, and call it from the route handler.

pr_title: Expand thin book topic maps
title: AllNoteRow is an exported type returned by listAllNotes, making it a client-facing/API-facing type.
body:
File: `apps/api/src/services/notes.ts`
Line: L859–L871
Issue: `AllNoteRow` is an exported type returned by `listAllNotes`, making it a client-facing/API-facing type. The schema package already exports `AllNote` (from `packages/schemas/src/notes.ts`) with the identical shape — both use string datetimes and the same fields. Keeping a local duplicate violates the shared-contract rule and will silently diverge.
Rule: CLAUDE.md § Non-Negotiable Engineering Rules
Suggested Fix: Replace `export type AllNoteRow = {...}` with `import type { AllNote } from '@eduagent/schemas'`. Update the `listAllNotes` return type to `Promise<{ notes: AllNote[]; nextCursor: string \
